title: Influence of Neighborhood Environments Features on Long-Term Changes in Physical Activity Across the Lifespan

key_numbers: 5 macro and 3 of 4 micro-scale baseline activity-supportive neighborhood features (and overall supportiveness) were...
Long-term follow-up ranged from 5.1 to 15 years in a combined follow-up sample of 1067.
Data came from four cross-sectional age-specific studies (children, adolescents, adults, older adults), with a fifth...
takeaways: MVPA declines from baseline to follow-up in all age groups.
Baseline activity-supportive neighborhood environment features are associated with less decline in MVPA across all age groups.
Specifically, “5 macro and 3 of 4 micro-scale” baseline activity-supportive features, and overall activity supportiveness, are associated with less MVPA decline.
